raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":0.7,"RX.4":0.3,"RX.5":0.3},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"rationale":{"R1.1":"The delegation to review the section centrally involves reframing the contribution claim, as the artifact shifts from multi-agent framework to prompting recipe in its conclusion.","R1.2":"Reviewing requires checking the logical consistency of the argument chain from method description to ablation-based conclusion.","R1.3":"The review must assess whether reported accuracies and ablation results align with the claims of performance gains and their drivers.","R1.4":"Assessing the novelty of VisionCrew against prior VLM prompting techniques is a central expected responsibility in reviewing this research section.","R1.6":"Polishing sentence-level grammar, flow, and conciseness is central to providing feedback on the writing quality of the section.","RX.1":"The review should centrally evaluate whether uncertainties, such as the tuned threshold or correlation observations, are adequately disclosed.","RX.2":"Avoiding overclaims is central, given the artifact's self-adjustment of the contribution from multi-agent to prompting.","RX.3":"Adhering to the scope of compositional VQA and frozen VLMs should be centrally checked in the review.","RX.4":"Considering downstream harms is peripheral but relevant for an AI system in visual reasoning, though not emphasized in the delegation.","RX.5":"Ensuring provenance of models like LLaVA-1.6 and datasets like GQA is peripheral to the review but supports traceability."}}

rationale.R1.1: The delegation to review the section centrally involves reframing the contribution claim, as the artifact shifts from multi-agent framework to prompting recipe in its conclusion.
rationale.R1.2: Reviewing requires checking the logical consistency of the argument chain from method description to ablation-based conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: The review must assess whether reported accuracies and ablation results align with the claims of performance gains and their drivers.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing the novelty of VisionCrew against prior VLM prompting techniques is a central expected responsibility in reviewing this research section.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing sentence-level grammar, flow, and conciseness is central to providing feedback on the writing quality of the section.
rationale.RX.1: The review should centrally evaluate whether uncertainties, such as the tuned threshold or correlation observations, are adequately disclosed.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims is central, given the artifact's self-adjustment of the contribution from multi-agent to prompting.
rationale.RX.3: Adhering to the scope of compositional VQA and frozen VLMs should be centrally checked in the review.
rationale.RX.4: Considering downstream harms is peripheral but relevant for an AI system in visual reasoning, though not emphasized in the delegation.
rationale.RX.5: Ensuring provenance of models like LLaVA-1.6 and datasets like GQA is peripheral to the review but supports traceability.
